etymology.description: 難(難) (nán, "difficult") + 忘(忘) (wàng, "forget") = hard to forget -> unforgettable
etymology.interpretation: The word 難忘(難忘) (nánwàng, "unforgettable") combines 難(難) (nán, "difficult") and 忘(忘) (wàng, "forget") to describe something that leaves a strong impression, making it difficult to erase from memory.

examples.chinese: 这次旅行非常难忘(這次旅行非常難忘)。
他给我留下了难忘的印象(他給我留下了難忘的印象)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gěi wǒ liúxiàle nánwàng de yìnxiàng.
examples.english: He left me with an unforgettable impression.

examples.chinese: 毕业典礼是一个难忘的时刻(畢業典禮是一個難忘的時刻)。
examples.pinyin: Bìyè diǎnlǐ shì yīgè nánwàng de shíkè.
examples.english: The graduation ceremony was a memorable moment.
